category_title: Active science leaders who connect school content with home-based animal care narratives
scenario_title: Navigating peer challenges and personal health narratives
minimal_context: The student is participating in a Lunchtime Science group discussion about asthma using science texts.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's start our group discussion about asthma using these science texts.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I can explain it to the group. The little pipes or airways get blocked and, well, you get it when you take out a basketball.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Kalisha just suggested that might just be from running too hard. What is your response?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I was just adding an idea to what you said earlier, sharing facts from my science class and my personal stories.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think about her point?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I get it once a year... It has gotten better... I don’t know. Kalisha is right! I don’t know if it is asthma or if I am out of shape. I don’t know any more.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's start our group discussion about asthma using these science texts.

STUDENT: I can explain it to the group. The little pipes or airways get blocked and, well, you get it when you take out a basketball.

TUTOR: Kalisha just suggested that might just be from running too hard. What is your response?

STUDENT: I was just adding an idea to what you said earlier, sharing facts from my science class and my personal stories.

TUTOR: What do you think about her point?

STUDENT: I get it once a year... It has gotten better... I don’t know. Kalisha is right! I don’t know if it is asthma or if I am out of shape. I don’t know any more.

task.learning_goal: Participating more productively in scientific discourse

context.materials: Newspaper articles, science texts, and specialists in the fields of midwifery and asthma research
context.social_setting: Group conversations with peers and instructors

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: After being challenged by peers during a group discussion
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Reflecting on her own medical condition, conceding to peer, and expressing uncertainty
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Uncertainty about her own medical condition
related_quotes: I get it once a year... It has gotten better... I don’t know. Kalisha is right! I don’t know if it is asthma or if I am out of shape. I don’t know any more.

behavior_patterns.condition: When interjecting her personal experience with exercise-induced asthma into a group discussion
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Sharing personal stories and explaining scientific mechanisms based on personal experience
related_quotes: The little pipes or airways get blocked and, well, you get it when you take out a basketball.
